Princess pink nails are beautiful because they are always in style and never go out of style. The main reason for this is that the trend can be changed and everyone loves it.

Celebrities like Mckenna Grace wearing the trend on the red carpet show that it is here to stay and will be a favorite among people who like how chic baby pink nails look.

articles.title: Bebe Launches Debut Betty Boop Collection in Bold Pop Culture Collaboration
articles.excerpt: Bebe celebrates its 50th anniversary with a playful new capsule collection featuring iconic cartoon character Betty Boop, blending vintage charm with modern glamour.
articles.content_section_1:
A Playful Fashion Collaboration Takes Center Stage

Fashion brand Bebe has officially stepped into the spotlight with its first-ever collaboration featuring the legendary cartoon icon Betty Boop. The debut Bebe x Betty Boop collection fuses two culturally powerful names, delivering a capsule designed for bold, fashion-forward women.

Known for its sultry, body-conscious aesthetic, Bebe reimagines Betty Boop through a more modern, elevated lens—transforming the vintage character into a symbol of contemporary glamour.
articles.content_section_2:
Reinventing an Icon for a New Generation

Betty Boop’s resurgence in pop culture—fueled in part by the success of Boop! The Musical on Broadway—has made her the perfect muse for a fresh fashion revival. The collection captures her playful, confident spirit while aligning it with Bebe’s signature sophistication.

Joey Gabbay, CEO of Bluestar Alliance, described the collaboration as a celebration of timeless style. By blending Betty’s classic iconography with modern design elements, the collection delivers a look that feels both nostalgic and undeniably current.
articles.content_section_3:
What’s Inside the Collection

The capsule includes a variety of pieces designed for both everyday wear and standout moments. From statement night-out outfits to relaxed denim, sleepwear, and wardrobe essentials, each piece incorporates bold prints, graphics, and motifs inspired by the world of Betty Boop.

The designs balance playful visuals with sleek silhouettes, making them versatile enough to transition from casual settings to evening looks.
articles.content_section_4:
A Milestone Moment for Bebe

The collection launched on March 24 exclusively online, marking a significant moment for Bebe as it celebrates its 50th anniversary. Founded in 1976, the brand has long been recognized for its chic, figure-flattering designs and its ability to blend day-to-night style seamlessly.

This collaboration is just one of several partnerships planned as part of the brand’s milestone year, highlighting its continued relevance at the intersection of fashion and pop culture.

articles.title: Alysa Liu Puts Her Signature Two-Toned Spin on a Classic LBD for the iHeartRadio Music Awards
articles.excerpt: Olympic gold medalist Alysa Liu elevates the classic little black dress with her signature edgy spin at the 2026 iHeartRadio Music Awards, blending minimalism with striking details.
articles.content_section_1:
A New Look for the Old LBD

Alysa Liu, a gold medalist at the Olympics and a cover star for Teen Vogue, wore a simple but bold little black dress to the 2026 iHeartRadio Music Awards. The look was both stylish and elegant, with a halter neckline that dipped into a cutout bodice with satin bra cups. The pencil skirt was asymmetrical and went down to the ankles. Liu wore black pointed-toe pumps with the dress and kept her accessories to a minimum so that the edgy halter straps and pinup-style neckline could stand out.
articles.content_section_2:
Minimalist Glam Meets Fame on the Red Carpet

Liu's glam was simple but powerful. Her halo hair was straight with a slight bend at the ends. She wore a soft smoky eye, peach blush, lip gloss, and a short black manicure that matched her dress. Liu has been making waves on the red carpet since she won a gold medal at the 2026 Winter Olympics in Italy. She wore a fancy Louis Vuitton mini dress to the Vanity Fair Oscar Party and a sleek tuxedo-inspired outfit to the Elton John AIDS Foundation Oscar Party. Alysa Liu is becoming a style icon in her own right, even when she's not on the ice.
articles.image1_source: Alysa Liu at iHeartRadio Music Awards 2026

articles.title: Freckles Emerge as a Standout Beauty Trend at Grammys 2026
articles.excerpt: From Billie Eilish to Olivia Dean, celebrities embraced natural freckles at the Grammys 2026, highlighting a shift toward minimal makeup and authentic self-expression.
articles.content_section_1:
A Subtle Yet Powerful Red Carpet Trend

A subtle yet powerful trend emerged on the red carpet at the 2026 Grammys: freckles. Initially, it was easy to overlook, but a closer examination of the evening's attendees showed a surprising embrace of natural skin texture. Many celebrities, it turned out, were celebrating their freckles, even choosing to let them shine on their faces.

This change is part of a bigger trend in beauty that values being real and unique. It means moving away from heavy coverage and toward more natural, skin-forward makeup looks.
articles.content_section_2:
Minimal Makeup, Maximum Impact

To show off their freckles, some artists chose soft glam and barely-there foundation. Billie Eilish wore a simple look with soft winged eyeliner, blurred lips, and a hint of warm blush that made her natural beauty stand out.

Sabrina Carpenter did something similar, using a dark pink lip, soft blush, and very little eye makeup so that her freckles could still be seen. Olivia Dean, on the other hand, used a bold lip to contrast her freckles, making a look that was both striking and balanced.

Lola Young and other artists have used freckles in their signature looks for a long time. Daniela and Lara, who are members of KATSEYE, also embraced the trend by not covering up their natural beauty marks.
articles.content_section_3:
The Rise of Natural Beauty

The trend for freckles is growing, just like the trends for glass skin and cloud skin before it. However, this time the focus is on self-expression. Freckles, whether real or fake, are now a big part of what makes modern beauty beautiful.

Earlier appearances, like Mckenna Grace at the Critics Choice Awards, showed that this trend was becoming more popular. Freckles will continue to be a big beauty trend in 2026, as more and more famous people wear them on the red carpet.

articles.title: Stand Oil's Everyday Purses Become It-Bags Thanks to Celebrity Influence
articles.excerpt: South Korean brand Stand Oil gains global attention as celebrities like Olivia Rodrigo and LE SSERAFIM’s Huh Yunjin turn its minimalist bags into viral must-haves.
articles.content_section_1:
If you are an avid shopper and have recently looked for a new bag, then you have probably heard of Stand Oil. This Korean brand has been slowly gaining popularity due to their trendy yet timeless designs that can easily blend in with any style. Not to mention, the stylish appeal of the brand has also garnered the attention of the world’s leading celebrities. In addition, the brand has also reached the height of TikTok popularity. Furthermore, the current popularity of the brand as an ‘it-bag’ creator has also reached new heights with the brand’s first-ever collaboration with none other than LE SSERAFIM’s Huh Yunjin.

The brand’s name is inspired by ‘stand oil,’ a thick and honey-colored linseed oil used in painting to achieve smooth, glossy, and long-lasting finishes. This inspires the brand to produce products that not only have current popularity but also stand the test of time. This is further explained by Yun Jo, the head of brand communications, stating, “In art, stand oil is used to achieve the finish, and it gets deeper and purer with time. We thought it would be great if our products could have that kind of quality.”
articles.content_section_2: She adds, “Rather than chasing fleeting trends, we aim to design bags that remain relevant and sophisticated over the years. When we launched the brand, we saw a gap in the market for accessories that combine thoughtful design, high quality, and affordable prices, all while feeling emotionally connected to daily life. Our mission has always been to create pieces that seamlessly fit into everyday routines while showcasing a distinctive yet understated style.”
articles.content_section_3:
Huh Yunjin’s connection to Stand Oil deepened during her first trip to the US with LE SSERAFIM. She was spotted carrying her Stand Oil bag decorated with ribbons, a small detail that inspired the collaboration. The collection features the mini Breezy bag, a smaller version of the brand’s popular style, in a “misty white” vegan leather shade, along with the Jenaissante charm set and scarf—embracing the bag charm trend.

Yunjin was initially drawn to the brand’s aesthetic. “In 2023, I bought a Stand Oil bag and styled it with a ribbon. I shared it on social media, and it unexpectedly went viral. Since then, I’ve built a strong relationship with Stand Oil, and that bag holds special meaning for me. When designing this piece, I kept that memory in mind and drew inspiration from how I styled it back then,” she shared.
articles.image1_source: Hankeon Hwang, courtesy of Stand Oil
articles.image2_source: Hankeon Hwang, courtesy of Stand Oil

articles.title: Spring Nail Ideas That Will Define 2026: Sage Green, Polka Dots & More
articles.excerpt: From glossy popsicle hues to sage green tones and playful polka dots, 2026’s spring nail trends blend minimal chic with bold, expressive designs.
articles.content_section_1:
The Rise of Nails as a Style Statement

The manicure is officially the smallest and most powerful accessory in fashion in 2026. No longer an afterthought, nail color, shape, and design are at the forefront of defining personal style. In fact, they can serve as the final touch that brings an entire look together.

Nail art has come a long way thanks to celebrity influences, social media crazes, and runway looks. From baby pink and midnight blue to milky sheens, chrome finishes, and jelly textures, the manicure is about individuality.

1. Popsicle Nails: Juicy Layers Take Over
articles.content_section_2:
This season, the nail art will be a delicious new look. Popsicle nail art, where the nail polish has a transparent look with colorful layers, will be the most popular. Think of cherry red, tangerine orange, watermelon pink, and electric raspberry all mixed together to make shiny, glass-like finishes.

The trend is very much about layering and abstract application, which gives the look of depth and makes it look almost edible. Tom Bachik, a famous nail artist, has already praised the style. Stars like Selena Gomez and Anna Cathcart have also embraced the juicy look on the red carpet.

2. Sage Green: The New Neutral
articles.content_section_3:
Dark greens are out; soft, herby sage tones are in for 2026. These soft, chalky greens give spring manicures a fresh, earthy look that is just the right mix of subtle and bold.

3. Polka Dot Nails: Playful Meets Classic
articles.content_section_4:
Polka dots are coming back in a big way, combining old-fashioned charm with new ideas. There are endless combinations, from classic black-and-white to bold ones like brown bases with pastel or neon dots.

Mixing patterns and layering designs, which artists like Queenie Nguyen made popular, gives this classic look a modern, stylish twist.

4. Cool Blue Nails: A Year-Round Favorite
articles.content_section_5:
Finishes like chrome and cat-eye effects make the look even better, so blue nails are good for any time of year and any event. Predictions like Pinterest Predicts have even made the trend last longer.

5. Butter Yellow: Soft Sunshine Style
articles.content_section_6:
Butter yellow nails are back, and they add a warm, creamy glow to manicures in the spring and summer. This happy but classy color goes well with both simple styles and fun nail art.

Butter yellow is a bright new color that feels both modern and classic, whether it's used with shiny finishes, matte textures, or small details like tiny dots.

articles.title: Jenna Ortega Stuns in Daring Glass Mosaic Dress on Late Show Appearance
articles.excerpt: Jenna Ortega turned heads in a striking Oscar de la Renta glass mosaic dress while sharing behind-the-scenes stories about working with Lady Gaga on Wednesday.
articles.content_section_1:
Jenna Ortega is known for making an entrance, and on this occasion, she literally sparkled on The Late Show With Stephen Colbert. The actress made waves in an jaw-dropping strapless red gown designed by Oscar de la Renta, made exclusively from glass shards. The gown was inspired by the beauty of stained glass and can be considered nothing less than a masterpiece.

Although the look was visually stunning, there was a sharp edge to this look. Ortega candidly admitted that the gown was not exactly the most comfortable choice for her and joked that on occasion, glass shards poked her when she sat down. “I feel uncomfortable and extremely comfortable at the same time,” she quipped, and that is exactly what this look was all about.
articles.content_section_2:
Apart from the stunning outfit, Ortega also gave fans a look at what it was like to work with Lady Gaga for the upcoming season of Wednesday. Ortega explained that the whole atmosphere changed the moment the pop star arrived on the set.

The regular filming location suddenly turned into a full-scale upgrade of the entire production, including the arrival of multiple trucks, a special walkway for the pop star, and even a change to the cast’s facilities. Ortega joked about how the regular setup of hers was replaced with luxury, including a barber’s chair and better refreshments, all to accommodate the global superstar.
articles.content_section_3:
Despite the glamour and luxury surrounding the pop star, Ortega explained how, behind the scenes, Lady Gaga was anything but intimidating. Ortega explained how the pop star was actually quite sweet, humble, and down-to-earth, despite being incredibly talented.

The look, of course, was not only a testament to Ortega’s growing status as a star but also a perfect blend of style, humor, and charm, proving that at times, the most memorable looks come with a little bit of discomfort and a whole lot of personality.
articles.image2_source: The Late Show With Stephen Colbert

articles.title: Stars Bring Glamour to the Oscars 2026 Red Carpet
articles.excerpt: From vibrant spring hues to timeless black and white ensembles, celebrities delivered standout fashion moments at the 98th Academy Awards.
articles.content_section_1:
The 98th Academy Awards brought together some of the biggest stars in Hollywood. The red carpet was a mix of bright colors and classic style. This year's fashion had a lot of spring-like colors, which made the event feel new and exciting.

Jessie Buckley made a statement in a regal ensemble, featuring a red, off-the-shoulder bodice paired with a sweeping pink ball skirt, courtesy of Chanel. Chase Infiniti, on the other hand, looked stunning in a lilac sleeveless gown by Louis Vuitton. The fitted bodice and cascading ruffles made a dramatic train.

Director Spike Lee, wore a bright purple hat and bow tie that added a splash of color to his outfit. He has worn this color before as a tribute to Prince.
articles.content_section_2:
Black and white ensembles made a strong showing on the red carpet, proving their enduring appeal. Rose Byrne, up for an award for her performance in If I Had Legs I'd Kick You, certainly understood the assignment, opting for a strapless black Dior gown. The dress was subtly enhanced with delicate white floral accents.

Emma Stone,dazzled in a floor-length, shimmering white gown from Louis Vuitton. The dress featured graceful cap sleeves and a streamlined silhouette. She was nominated for Bugonia.
articles.content_section_3:
Teyana Taylor, who stood out all awards season, wore a black and white feathered gown by Chanel that was another memorable look. The sleeveless style had a sheer panel that gave the classic color scheme a modern twist.

The Oscars 2026 red carpet was a mix of bold experimentation and classic style. Celebrities wore bright seasonal colors and elegant monochrome looks.

articles.title: Zendaya’s Bixie Cut Steals the Spotlight at Oscars 2026
articles.excerpt: Zendaya showcases the bixie cut at the Oscars 2026, cementing the hybrid hairstyle as one of the biggest hair trends to watch this year.
articles.content_section_1:
When Zendaya showed off her version of the bixie cut at the 2026 Oscars, she started a new beauty trend. The hairstyle is already getting a lot of attention, and after the actor showed off a few different ways to wear it in just one week, it is now being called one of the year's most important hair trends.

A pixie and a bob make up the bixie cut. It has short layers and a soft, textured look. It has the shape of a bob and the easy, slightly messy look of a pixie, which makes it both trendy and useful.
articles.content_section_2:
Zendaya wore her bixie cut straight with a side part and soft bangs to the Oscars. It looked smooth and shiny. She put some of her hair behind her ears and flicked the ends out at the back to show off the cut's layers. She still looked classy on the red carpet.

The bixie demonstrated its versatility, proving it could be both elegant and playful.
articles.content_section_3:
At ESSENCE Black Women in Hollywood Awards 2026, held earlier this week, Zendaya debuted a fresh style, curling her bixie cut into soft, elegant waves. Her bangs were curled into soft ringlets that framed her face and made it look romantic and textured.

The bixie cut is a new twist on popular layered hairstyles like wolf cuts and modern bobs. It has a nice balance of structure and movement, so it's a stylish and easy-to-care-for choice for 2026.

articles.title: Zendaya Stuns in Bridal-Inspired Louis Vuitton at ‘The Drama’ Paris Premiere
articles.excerpt: Zendaya turns heads at The Drama Paris premiere in a striking custom Louis Vuitton gown, continuing her bridal-themed press tour with a dramatic backless silhouette and bold black bow detail.
articles.content_section_1:
At the Paris premiere of The Drama, Zendaya had one of the most talked-about red carpet moments of the year. She wore a custom white Louis Vuitton gown that was both simple and dramatic, thanks to Law Roach's styling. The front of the dress had a sleek column shape, and the back had a dramatic open design with a big black bow and a flowing train.

The look was also described as a "wedding cake topper" due to the mix of sculptural elegance and bridal inspiration. The outfit was both modern and memorable due to the clean white fabric and the bold use of black accents.
articles.content_section_2:
Zendaya is continuing to employ her "method dressing" look during the movie's press tour. The drama film, which stars Robert Pattinson, is about a couple who is going through a crazy wedding week.

Her Paris look was the "something new" part of a traditional wedding theme, following earlier looks that were inspired by other wedding traditions. This consistent styling approach has made her red carpet choices more interesting and full of stories.
articles.content_section_3:
Zendaya finished off the look with black pumps that matched the bow detail and high-end jewelry diamonds from David Morris. Her hair and makeup stayed clean and up-to-date, which kept the dress the main focus.

The bridal-inspired look has also sparked rumors about her personal life, especially her relationship with Tom Holland, since she has been seen wearing a gold ring on her ring finger. Even though nothing has been confirmed, the fashion story keeps people talking. Overall, the look made Zendaya look like one of Hollywood's most powerful and strategic style icons.

articles.title: Taylor Swift Wins Artist of the Year at 2026 iHeartRadio Awards
articles.excerpt: Taylor Swift wins Artist of the Year at the 2026 iHeartRadio Music Awards and urges fans to nurture their hobbies and passions, reflecting on her journey from a young songwriter to a global music icon.
articles.content_section_1:
Swift Honored at the iHeartRadio Music Awards

Taylor Swift won Artist of the Year at the 2026 iHeartRadio Music Awards. Olympic gold medalist Alysa Liu gave the award at the Dolby Theatre in Los Angeles on March 26. Swift, who is 36 years old, won seven awards in all. In her acceptance speech, she talked about how important it is to have passion, practice, and perseverance when trying to improve at something. She praised Liu for her hard work and the happiness she brings to the world, connecting it to the goals of the people in the audience.
articles.content_section_2:
Advice to Fans: Nurture Your Hobby

In her touching speech, Swift talked about her childhood and how writing songs went from being a hobby to being the most important thing in her life. She stressed how important it is to spend time learning, practicing, and making mistakes on your own before asking for validation from others. Swift told her fans to work on their own crafts away from the stress of social media. She said, "Give yourself time to make mistakes, give yourself time to hone your craft." She ended by thanking the audience for letting her turn her hobby into a job and wishing them success in their creative work.
articles.content_section_3:
Recognizing Other Nominees

Nominees for Artist of the Year included Bad Bunny, Benson Boone, Chris Brown, Jelly Roll, Kendrick Lamar, Lady Gaga, Morgan Wallen, Sabrina Carpenter, and Tate McRae, highlighting the competitive field of talented artists in 2026.
articles.image1_source: Travis Kelce and Taylor Swift at the iHeartRadio Music Awards in Los Angeles on March 26, 2026. Christopher Polk/Billboard via Getty

articles.title: Netflix Documentary Explores Microplastics and Health Risks
articles.excerpt: Netflix’s upcoming documentary ‘The Plastic Detox’ investigates the impact of microplastics on health, fertility, and daily life, while highlighting sustainable innovations in fashion and materials.
articles.content_section_1:
On March 16, Netflix will release a new documentary called "The Plastic Detox". The movie looks into the growing concern about microplastics, especially their possible links to lower fertility rates and other health issues that may come up later.

Microplastics have also been found in clothing fibers and cosmetics, in addition to food packaging and toys. The word describes small pieces of plastic that can come apart from bigger pieces over time and be eaten or breathed in. More and more studies have found a connection between plastics, which often have chemicals added during the manufacturing process, and health problems, from infertility to cancer.

The documentary also looks at whether making small changes to your lifestyle can help lower your exposure and improve your health in general.
articles.content_section_2:
With the help of environmental and reproductive epidemiologist Shanna Swan, the documentary follows six couples who are having trouble getting pregnant for no clear reason. Over the course of three months, they try to cut down on their exposure to chemicals that come from plastic in the hopes of getting healthier.

The film features voices from community organizers opposing petrochemical projects, alongside fashion designers striving to eliminate harmful dyes and synthetic fibers from their manufacturing methods.

The documentary connects environmental problems with personal health, showing how plastics are everywhere in modern life.
articles.content_section_3:
LVMH-Backed Sparxell Showcased in Documentary for Innovative Plant-Based Pigment Solutions

One of the documentary's highlights is its focus on Sparxell, a company developing plant-based, biodegradable pigments. Sparxell is working to supplant conventional petrochemical dyes, mined minerals, and plastic-based pigments—think of the widely used azo dyes—with assistance from LVMH.

The company's cellulose-based pigments are already being used in business, and there are several partnerships and pilot projects going on. The documentary also shows how designers like Patrick McDowell have worked with Sparxell and textile innovators to make plant-based alternatives for making clothes.The movie highlights these new ideas to show how they could help make plastics less harmful to the environment and people's health.

By spotlighting these innovations, the film underscores the potential for sustainable solutions to reduce the environmental and health impacts of plastics.

articles.title: Yves Saint Laurent Onstage Exhibition Explores Designer's Performing Arts Passion
articles.excerpt: The Yves Saint Laurent Onstage exhibition in Marrakech highlights the designer’s deep connection to theatre, ballet, and music hall through costumes, sketches, and archival works.
articles.content_section_1:
The Yves Saint Laurent Onstage exhibition was opened in the Musée Yves Saint Laurent Marrakech in late January. The exhibition is centered around Yves Saint Laurent's work in theater, music halls, and ballet. The exhibition will be open until January 5, 2027. It provides a new perspective on a lesser-known but important side of Yves Saint Laurent's work.

This exhibition features Yves Saint Laurent's lasting love for the performing arts, which was sparked in him when he was just thirteen years old in Oran, Algeria. Curated by Stephan Janson and Domitille Éblé from the Musée Yves Saint Laurent Paris, the show explores how stage performance influenced his artistic vision. Building on the 2024 Rome exhibition, the Marrakech edition features an ambitious set design and previously unseen pieces, using items from the Fondation Pierre Bergé - Yves Saint Laurent archives and international collections.
articles.content_section_2: Saint Laurent is known to be a visionary costume designer. He revolutionized women's fashion by highlighting movement, freedom, and character expression. The artistic works created were not only for decoration but were incorporated into the performances and became a vital part of the artists' identities. The works ensured that artists such as Sylvie Vartan and Johnny Hallyday became more captivating on stage, which is what led to their legendary success.
articles.content_section_3:
The exhibition offers visitors a realistic setting to walk around and view original costumes, sketches, and set designs, including rare archival pieces. Some of the works were created by the famous Karinska workshops, thus showing how sketches can be transformed into living costumes inspired by Léon Bakst's bright colors.

The Yves Saint Laurent museum has been open since 2017 and is located in the heart of Marrakech, near the Jardin Majorelle. It continues to honor the designer's lasting cultural impact.

articles.title: From High Street to Halftime: Bad Bunny Debuts Custom Zara at Super Bowl 2026
articles.excerpt: Bad Bunny made history at Super Bowl 2026 by wearing custom Zara designs during his halftime performance, marking a major cultural and strategic moment for Inditex in the U.S. market.
articles.content_section_1:
Inditex showed how important the US is as a growth market when its main brand, Zara, dressed halftime performer Bad Bunny at Super Bowl LX on February 8, 2026. The event, which took place at Levi's Stadium in Santa Clara, drew millions of viewers and put Zara at the center of one of the world's biggest cultural events.

The Seattle Seahawks won the game against the New England Patriots, but the Puerto Rican superstar stole the show at halftime. Bad Bunny gave a performance that mixed music with a strong cultural message, just like Michael Jackson, Beyoncé, and Kendrick Lamar.
articles.content_section_2:
Bad Bunny, whose real name is Benito Antonio Martínez Ocasio, opened with his hit "Tití Me Preguntó." He turned the stage into a tribute to Puerto Rican culture, complete with images of sugarcane fields. Lady Gaga and Ricky Martin, two of the biggest stars in the world, were also there, which made the show even more appealing to people all over the world.

The artist ended the show with a touchdown, and the football was emblazoned with the phrase "Together, We Are America." This is a very powerful symbol that emphasizes unity and Hispanic-American history. The performance combined cultural pride with a political statement.

Being featured by Zara was a big deal for the fashion world because Zara is one of the few Spanish brands that has designed for a halftime show for the Super Bowl. Zara designed two custom off-white ensembles for Bad Bunny.
articles.content_section_3:
The first look, called the "Varsity" look, had a jersey sweatshirt, pants, and tie that were inspired by the NFL. The number 64 and the name Ocasio were on the shirt as a tribute to his mother. The second suit, called a "Power" suit, had big shoulders and loose seams. It was worn during the main part of the performance.

The single color scheme reflected Caribbean influence and helped to reinforce the artist's message of unity. The Adidas "BadBo 1.0" was the artist's first original shoe shape for the brand and completed the look.

The Inditex deal was more than just a fashion collaboration for the retailer; it was a shrewd business move to increase its cultural and business footprint in the U.S., which has been its largest market outside of Spain since 2021.

articles.title: Charlie Puth Enters His ‘Dad-Music Era’—And He’s Loving Every Minute of It
articles.excerpt: With his new album ‘Whatever’s Clever!’, Charlie Puth embraces authenticity, fatherhood, and a nostalgic ‘Yacht Rock 2026’ sound—marking a deeply personal new chapter.
articles.content_section_1:
Charlie Puth is stepping into a brand-new era—and it’s not what you’d expect from the pop hitmaker behind chart-toppers like “See You Again.” This time, it’s personal. After Taylor Swift famously declared that “Charlie Puth should be a bigger artist” on her album The Tortured Poets Department, the comment didn’t just go viral—it sparked something deeper.

“I just went ahead and made an album which I happened to be so proud of,” Puth revealed. That album, Whatever’s Clever!, drops March 27 and signals a bold creative shift. Think less polished pop formulas and more raw, nostalgic energy—what he calls “Yacht Rock 2026.”
articles.content_section_2:
This new sound isn’t just a stylistic pivot—it’s a reflection of who Puth is becoming. With the arrival of his son, Jude, the artist's music seems to be mirroring the changes in his life, in ways he hadn't anticipated. “It’s music I would listen to as a dad,” he says, embracing the label with a sense of pride.

Gone are the days of chasing trends or trying to fit into a mold. Puth candidly admits that earlier in his career, he experimented with his image and identity just to be heard. Now, he’s done with that. “I didn’t need to mold myself into something… I just had to be me the entire time.”
articles.content_section_3:
This honesty is evident throughout the album. Tracks like "I Used to Be Cringe" and "Don't Meet Your Heroes" explore vulnerability, self-discovery, and the unflinching reality of growing up in the public eye. Rather than resorting to vague songwriting, Puth goes for specificity, as he explains, "The more personal the story, the more people can relate to it."

His Super Bowl LX performance, even more recent, was laden with emotional resonance. He incorporated elements that paid tribute to Whitney Houston's iconic rendition of the national anthem from the 2012 Super Bowl. This blend of respect, growth, and individuality defines the artist's present.

With nothing to prove and everything to express, Charlie Puth’s so-called “dad-music era” might be his most honest—and best—yet.

articles.title: V&A Celebrates Elsa Schiaparelli’s Legacy with ‘Fashion Becomes Art’ Exhibition
articles.excerpt: London’s V&A museum unveils a major exhibition dedicated to Elsa Schiaparelli, showcasing her groundbreaking fusion of fashion, art, and surrealism across decades.
articles.content_section_1:
Elsa Schiaparelli may not be as well-known as Coco Chanel or Christian Dior, but she had just as big of an effect on fashion. Now, London's Victoria and Albert Museum (V&A) is giving her the attention she deserves.

The new show, "Schiaparelli: Fashion Becomes Art," will be open from March 28 to November 8. It provides great detail about the history of Maison Schiaparelli and how she revolutionized fashion by adding art, performance, and surrealism to it. Her work was not just about clothing; it was about challenging the possibilities of fashion.
articles.content_section_2:
The exhibition features more than 400 pieces, including 100 outfits and 50 pieces of art. It will take you on a journey to experience the creative world of Schiaparelli. It has everything from jewelry and accessories to paintings and furniture. It also includes perfumes and rare artifacts from the Philadelphia Museum of Art and Schiaparelli's archives.

The exhibition has been curated by Sonnet Stanfill, Lydia Caston, and Rosalind McKever. The exhibition will cover the period from the 1920s until the present day. The exhibition will also show the innovative work of the past and how it is applicable in the present day by also showcasing the work of the current creative director, Daniel Roseberry.
articles.content_section_3:
The famous Skeleton dress and Tears dress of 1938, as well as the surrealist shoe hat, are some of the best works of the designer. All of these works were done with the help of artist Salvador Dalí. Along with the works of other famous artists like Pablo Picasso, Jean Cocteau, Man Ray, and Eileen Agar, these works also show the strong connection between the fashion world of Schiaparelli and the world of art.

The exhibition also connects the past with the present by ending with dramatic modern looks worn by stars like Ariana Grande and Dua Lipa, which have been designed by Roseberry. Schiaparelli did not just make clothes; she also made fashion a place for ideas, imagination, and the extraordinary, according to V&A director Tristram Hunt.

articles.title: From Molly Exhibition to Laopu Gold: Shanghai Scene
articles.excerpt: Shanghai’s cultural and fashion scene is buzzing with new exhibitions, innovative galleries, and luxury brand openings, highlighting art, design, and lifestyle trends in 2026.
articles.content_section_1:
Art and Toy Culture: The Molly Exhibition & Place M

Pop Mart is returning to Shanghai with a brand-new Molly exhibition. Last year’s Labubu and Crybaby exhibitions have been a huge success. This exhibition is centered around Molly, the first signed and proprietary character of Pop Mart. It has collaborated with 21 artists and local artisans in the field of collectible toys and modern art.

Last year, Place M, an independent photography gallery established by two of Japan’s most renowned photographers, Daido Moriyama and Masato Seto, in the 1980s, officially opened in China. This latest exhibition of theirs is about the physical diversity of fashion photography.
articles.content_section_2:
Luxury & Fashion Highlights: Laopu Gold and Lemaire

Laopu Gold, the trendy Chinese-style gold jewelry brand, has officially opened the doors to their flagship store in the exclusive Plaza 66 in Shanghai. The event was met with the same hype as a Supreme store opening. Despite the current slump in the price of gold, the brand has managed to expand their product line while providing the same high-quality in-store experience as the best high-end fashion brands.

Japanese fashion brand Nanamica has officially opened the doors to their latest store on Wukang Road, which founder Eiichiro Homma describes as the 'Daikanyama of China.' The 750-square-foot L-shaped store features a navy tile exterior, as well as a wooden cabin interior, which provides a 'beach house in the city' feel.

articles.title: H&M Earnings Seen Rising Despite Expected Sales Decline
articles.excerpt: H&M is projected to post higher profits for its first quarter even as sales decline, driven by cost discipline, improved margins, and shifting global demand patterns.
articles.content_section_1:
Hennes & Mauritz is getting ready to release its first-quarter earnings report. Sales are expected to go down, but profits should tell a more positive story. Analysts say that net profit will go up from 585 million kronor a year ago to 647 million kronor for the period from December to February.

Even though sales are expected to drop by 8.7% to 50.54 billion kronor, the company is still expected to make more money. This shows that the retailer is focused on being efficient and keeping costs down in a tough global retail environment.
articles.content_section_2:
There were a number of reasons why sales were expected to be lower. A strong Black Friday in late November brought forward demand, which made sales slow down in December. More problems came from careful inventory planning in the U.S., the changing date of the Lunar New Year, and weak consumer demand in major European markets.

However, February is expected to show signs of recovery, thanks to easier comparisons to the same month last year and a return to normal seasonal demand. In terms of local currency, overall sales growth is expected to stay about the same, but currency headwinds, especially a stronger Swedish krona, are likely to make the numbers look worse than they are.
articles.content_section_3:
On the other hand, margins are likely to get better. Analysts think that gross margin could rise to 50.0%, up from 49.1% last year. This is because shipping costs are going down, the supply chain is becoming more efficient, and the U.S. dollar is getting weaker. This improvement is expected to make up for the rising pressure from tariffs.

Operating profits are expected to increase to 1.33 billion kronor, and the operating margin is expected to increase to 2.6%. The investors will focus on what the company has to say regarding key areas, such as the U.S., where tariffs are an area of concern, and the Middle East, where performance is an area of concern. The earnings report has the potential to alter the perception of the company’s financial stability in the face of challenges across the world. The company’s shares have increased by nearly 29% over the past year.

articles.title: The Global Rise of Second-Hand Fashion: From Stigma to Style Staple
articles.excerpt: Second-hand fashion is no longer an afterthought. Once tied to charity shops, preloved clothing is now a cultural and economic force, driven by sustainability, creativity, and smart consumer choices.
articles.content_section_1:
However, fashion is currently undergoing a fundamental shift in perception. What used to be considered the stepchild of the fashion industry has now come to define one of the largest growth stories of the last decade. Second-hand fashion is no longer the epitome of shame and guilt; rather, it has come to define the epitome of creativity and smart consumerism.

The shift in second-hand fashion has not only been quantitative but also qualitative. A generation of consumers has grown up with the idea of fast fashion and has now come to define the shift to slower fashion cycles, not just in the context of the season but also in the context of individuality.
articles.content_section_2:
The second-hand fashion industry has undergone a fundamental shift in perception and has now come to define one of the fastest-growing markets in the world of fashion. While second-hand fashion has traditionally come to define the world of charity shops and thrift stores, it has now come to define the world of the mainstream, thanks to the power of technology and the ideals of circular fashion and sustainability.

This is reflected in consumer behavior too: in Europe and North America, substantial percentages of people have purchased second-hand apparel, accessories, or footwear within the past 12 months. Though there is some discrepancy in what is being measured – for instance, some focus on second-hand clothing only, while others focus on accessories and footwear too, and some focus on gross merchandise volume instead of retail replacement value – there is no doubt that there is double-digit growth in all areas.
articles.content_section_3:
Globally, second-hand apparel is projected to be worth USD 367 billion by 2029, growing nearly three times faster than the wider apparel market. The largest country market is the U.S., and this is projected to be worth USD 74 billion. This comes after a strong 14% growth in 2024. Other areas, such as Europe, where platforms such as Vinted and Depop are performing well and benefiting from positive regulatory developments on reuse and recycling.

Second-hand luxury retail, once considered a threat to luxury’s exclusivity, is now an integral part of the global luxury landscape. According to UniformMarket, second-hand luxury retail is projected to reach USD 66 billion in 2030. The second-hand apparel market is conservatively projected to reach USD 250-400 billion in 2030, with second-hand luxury retail adding another USD 50-70 billion to that figure.

articles.title: From Sensory Stress to Satisfaction: A Guide to Making Retail More Neuro-Inclusive
articles.excerpt: Creating a neuro-inclusive shopping environment improves experiences for neurodivergent customers while benefiting all visitors, combining accessibility, thoughtful design, and staff awareness.
articles.content_section_1:
Rethinking Retail for Neurodivergent Shoppers

The retail industry tends to ignore the way neurodivergent individuals perceive their retail environment. While a positive retail environment can increase sales for all, the positive retail environment for neurodivergent individuals can vary significantly from the traditional retail environment. FashionUnited explores the ways in which retailers can provide a neuro-inclusive retail environment.

Accessibility in retail has gained more importance in recent times, with the introduction of the European Digital Accessibility Act in June 2025, which focuses on digital accessibility in e-commerce. Neuro-inclusive retail is in its development stage due to a lack of understanding. Experts have estimated that one in five individuals are neurodivergent, indicating the potential for businesses to provide accommodating retail environments.

Overstimulation in retail can be caused by auto-playing videos, pop-ups, or excessive text in retail environments. Tactile sensitivity can also be a problem in the fashion industry, where itchy fabrics, constrictive clothing, or scratchy tags can be overwhelming. Neuro-inclusive retail can help to reduce these overstimulations in retail environments, reducing the need to return items. Not only is it good for business, but it is also a compassionate act to ensure neurodivergent individuals feel appreciated.
articles.content_section_2:
Practical Approaches to Neuro-Inclusive Retail

There have been several approaches by various retailers and cities to ensure sensory-friendly environments. For example, supermarkets have quiet hours, while Wiesbaden has dimmed lighting and sounds in its city center, involving 20 participating retailers. The O2 Centre in London has also set up a sensory-friendly room called 'Safe Space.'

Lego has provided neurodivergent children with a 'sensory pack' consisting of sunglasses, fidget toys, headphones, and an emotion card. According to expert Saskia Schepers, although such efforts are appreciated, they only focus on solving issues caused by overstimulation in the first place.

Staff Training and Awareness

Awareness among retail staff is essential in ensuring an inclusive retail environment. Understanding non-visible disabilities through the sunflower lanyard scheme enables them to modify how they communicate. De Warmste Entree, a Belgian initiative, has provided retail staff with training in how to effectively engage with customers who have social interaction difficulties. Sephora has also set up colored baskets for customers who need assistance, in addition to self-service checkouts.

Environmental changes also have an important role to play. Disruptive lighting and noise, as well as eliminating visual chaos and controlling smells, can greatly enhance the overall shopping experience. The creation of 'breathing space' and visual 'breathing points' is essential to avoid sensory overload. Neuro-inclusive design is also beneficial for neurotypical visitors.

Schepers recommends taking it one step at a time. Reducing noise, eliminating chaos, and creating space are all simple steps to take in creating a neuro-inclusive retail space.

articles.title: Why the Market Is Betting on Lasting Value in Fine Gold Necklaces
articles.excerpt: Rising gold prices, shifting consumer priorities, and a focus on sustainability are driving strong demand for fine gold necklaces as both fashion statements and long-term investments.
articles.content_section_1:
A Market Shift Toward Lasting Value

The fine jewelry market is in an era of tremendous change as it transitions from disposable fashion accessories to long-lasting investment pieces. At the heart of this revolution is the fine gold necklace, appreciated for its durability, timelessness, and wearability.

The surge in gold prices in 2025 has reshaped how people shop. Unlike in the past, the high prices have actually sparked a greater interest in purchasing enduring, quality necklaces. Fine necklaces, especially those made from solid 14k gold, have become increasingly appealing to consumers.

The global jewelry market is set to reach an impressive $374 billion by 2025. The fine and demi-fine segment is increasingly contributing to this growth. As consumers increasingly reject plated products, brands in the accessible fine segment are experiencing remarkable growth.
articles.content_section_2:
Layering, Personalization, and Evolving Style Trends

Today, the consumer is not just buying a unique statement piece, but rather a jewelry wardrobe. Layering necklaces has become a major fashion statement that allows the consumer to be unique in their style expression by mixing and matching different lengths, textures, and types of pendants.

The single metal statement has also become a less popular fashion statement in the fine necklace market. Mixing metals, or combining gold, silver, and rose tones, has become a popular fashion statement that presents a new business opportunity for the industry.
articles.content_section_3:
From Runway Influence to Retail Innovation

The runway has significantly influenced the fine necklace market. Choker styles and tight-fitting necklaces in white gold and diamonds are gaining popularity, while pendant-focused necklaces remain a consistent bestseller at every price point.

Modular jewelry has dramatically reshaped the retail world. This concept allows consumers to purchase components, like a pendant, separately from the chain, effectively letting them personalize their jewelry and curate a collection.
articles.content_section_4:
Sustainability and Transparency Take Center Stage

In 2026, purchasing is no longer limited to visual appeal. Sourcing, production, and sustainability are at the forefront of consumers' minds. Traceability is a must-have for brands, providing transparency about gold sourcing, mining, and labor.

The emergence of lab-grown diamonds is changing the industry. This alternative is more cost-effective and environmentally friendly. Larger diamonds in unique colors are now feasible thanks to technology. This is creating a trend among consumers to add this type of stone to their fine necklace collections.
articles.content_section_5:
The Future of Fine Necklace Investment

With gold prices remaining elevated and consumers wanting to make meaningful purchases that last, investing in a fine gold necklace is a double-edged sword. Consumers are investing in items that are both fashionable and allow for self-expression.

For retailers and brands, success in 2026 will come down to creating cohesive collections that are transparent and flexible. The world of fine necklaces is no longer about aesthetics; it's about value, transparency, and staying relevant in a rapidly changing world.
